item.title: 🇫🇮🏊 Finland Joins Nordic Boycott of World Aquatics Over Russia Participation
item.description: Finland announced it will not host international swimming championships while World Aquatics allows Russian and Belarusian athletes to compete, joining Norway and Poland in a growing Nordic protest against the governing body.

item.title: 🇬🇧👑 King Charles III to Visit US Amid Iran War Tensions
item.description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla begin a four-day state visit to the US on Monday, seeking to strengthen US-UK ties despite tensions over Britain refusing to support the Iran war. The visit follows Queen Elizabeth IIs 1991 precedent of addressing Congress.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Rejects Direct Talks with US as Pakistan Mediation Continues
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi laid out Iran's demands in Islamabad while ruling out face-to-face meetings with US negotiators. The US has dispatched envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Pakistan for indirect peace talks.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Major Russian Attack Kills Seven in Ukraine as Dnipro Struck by Barrage
item.description: Russia launched over 660 drones and missiles in a massive overnight attack that killed at least seven people and wounded dozens in Dnipro and other Ukrainian regions. It marks one of the largest Russian barrages in recent weeks.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Four Killed in Israeli Strikes on Southern Lebanon Despite Ceasefire
item.description: Israeli strikes killed four people in southern Lebanon, state media reported, while the Israeli military said Hezbollah had fired rockets at Israel. The attacks come despite an extended ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine and Russia Swap 193 Prisoners Each in UAE-Facilitated Exchange
item.description: Russia and Ukraine exchanged 193 prisoners of war each in a swap facilitated by the United States and UAE. The returned prisoners included soldiers, border guards, and police, some with injuries.

item.title: 🛢️ Macron Pushes to Reopen Strait of Hormuz as TotalEnergies Warns of Shortages
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron reaffirmed efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz amid the Iran conflict. TotalEnergies CEO warned of global energy shortages if the war continues for months.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Republicans Retool Midterm Strategy Amid Trump Approval Slide
item.description: With gas prices up and Trump's approval ratings down amid the Iran war, Republicans are recalibrating their midterm election blueprint to focus on policy rather than personality.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Sanctions Chinese Refinery Over Iranian Oil Purchases
item.description: The Trump administration imposed sanctions on an independent Chinese refinery for buying billions of dollars worth of Iranian oil, even as peace talks continue over the weekend.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iranian Drone Boat Captured by US in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US forces captured an Iranian unmanned surface vessel in the Strait of Hormuz, escalating tensions in the vital waterway amid ongoing conflict.

item.title: ⚔️ US Navy Rushing Hellfire Missiles to Carrier Groups for Counter-Drone Defense
item.description: The US Navy is accelerating deployment of Hellfire missiles to carrier strike groups in the Middle East to counter the growing drone threat from Iranian forces.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia Suspected of Spying on Sweden via Church Near Airport
item.description: Swedish security services are investigating a Russian church near Stockholm airport for suspected espionage activities, amid growing concerns about Russian intelligence operations in the Nordic region.

item.title: 🇧🇷 Flavio Bolsonaro Visits Lula to Discuss Brazil Election
item.description: Flavio Bolsonaro, son of former president Jair Bolsonaro, visited President Lula to discuss the upcoming election, in an unusual meeting between political rivals.

item.title: 🇵🇰 Islamabad Residents Bear Cost of Pakistan US-Iran Mediation
item.description: Pakistani citizens face economic and security costs as their government mediates between the US and Iran in ceasefire negotiations.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel Strikes Gaza as Ceasefire Negotiations Continue
item.description: Israeli forces conducted strikes on Gaza while ceasefire negotiations remain ongoing, with both sides trading blame for the continued violence.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel Warns Lebanon Over Hezbollah Rocket Fire
item.description: Israel warned Lebanon after Hezbollah launched rockets toward northern Israel, with tensions rising despite the extended ceasefire agreement.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel Targets Syrian Military Sites in Overnight Strikes
item.description: Israeli forces conducted overnight strikes on Syrian military positions, escalating regional tensions amid the broader Middle East conflict.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israeli Airstrike Kills Palestinian in Gaza
item.description: An Israeli airstrike killed one Palestinian in Gaza as fighting continues despite international calls for de-escalation.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iranian Forces Capture US Drone Boat in Hormuz
item.description: Iranian forces captured a US unmanned surface vessel in the Strait of Hormuz, further escalating tensions in the critical waterway.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israeli Military on High Alert Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: Israel Defense Forces remain on heightened alert as regional tensions with Iran and Hezbollah continue to escalate.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukrainian Drone Strikes Hit Russian Oil Facilities
item.description: Ukrainian drone strikes continued to target Russian energy infrastructure as the conflict between Kyiv and Moscow intensifies.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Calls for EU to Shut Down Russian Crypto Schemes
item.description: Ukraine urged the European Union to target Russian cryptocurrency operations in the next round of sanctions against Moscow.

item.title: 🇺🇦 EU Sanctions Russian Cultural Figures Over Crimea Archaeological Looting
item.description: The European Union sanctioned Russian cultural figures accused of participating in illegal archaeological excavations in occupied Crimea.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Gives Final Approval to 90 Billion Euro Ukraine Loan and 20th Russia Sanctions Package
item.description: The European Union formally approved a 90 billion euro loan package for Ukraine alongside its 20th round of sanctions against Russia.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Ambassadors Back 20th Russia Sanctions Package After Hungary, Slovakia Drop Veto
item.description: EU ambassadors approved the 20th sanctions package against Russia after Hungary and Slovakia withdrew their objections.
